method_atom: A one-parameter family of models whose distinct limiting regimes (reached by tuning a control parameter across its range) yield structures that share the same categorical/homological invariants yet are not connected by any direct geometric transformation, so that equivalence is established at the level of abstract invariants rather than by a point-to-point map. The correspondence between the regimes is organized by a general duality principle relating the limiting structures, and those limits may be generalized objects — quotient/orbifold-type or noncommutative deformations — rather than ordinary smooth spaces.
object_atom: Abelian gauged linear sigma models — two-dimensional supersymmetric abelian gauge theories, mainly for complete intersections of quadric hypersurfaces but also more general targets — together with their differing Kahler phases and the conformal field theories they realize.
brief_reason: What transfers is the idea that separate limiting regimes of a tunable family can carry identical categorical/homological invariants without a direct geometric map, unified by a duality principle; the GLSMs, quadric-intersection targets, Kahler phases, and CFTs are the physics/geometry-specific object.
